objections.description: Using 'cautious heuristics' to determine legal validity creates unacceptable professional liability risk. Legal status requires authoritative data (e.g., official consolidated acts from Planalto or LexML), not client-side text inference. False confidence in normative status—such as incorrectly displaying a revoked act as valid—can lead to malpractice. The current implementation risks misleading users into citing invalid legislation.
objections.suggested_fix: Remove heuristic inference entirely. Replace with explicit tri-state logic: 'Vigente (consolidado oficial)', 'Revogado (consolidado oficial)', or 'Status não verificado - requer verificação manual'. Integrate official consolidated legislation feeds or clearly mark unverified documents rather than guessing.

objections.description: Professional legal audit work requires cross-device continuity and immutable audit trails (who searched what, when, for which case matter). localStorage is ephemeral, device-bound, and insecure for compliance purposes. The current 'recent history' implementation breaks workflow continuity when users switch devices or clear browser data, and provides no provenance for research methodology.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement authenticated user sessions with server-side Project/Workspace entities. Persist searches, reading positions, and annotations per-project with versioning and exportable audit logs. localStorage should only be a fallback for unauthenticated users, not the primary persistence strategy.

objections.description: Hiding the mobile navigation on document routes breaks the core professional workflow of iterative search-and-review. Legal researchers frequently switch between search results, multiple open documents, and cross-references. Forcing browser back-button navigation loses scroll position, filter state, and mental context, increasing cognitive load and research time.
objections.suggested_fix: Retain persistent bottom navigation on mobile document routes. Reposition the mobile action bar to sit above the nav or collapse into a floating action button rather than replacing system navigation. Preserve search state (filters, pagination) in session storage to enable seamless return to result lists.

objections.description: Displaying backend query interpretation (interpreted_query, inferred_filters) without user override capability creates a 'transparency trap.' Users can see the system misunderstood their intent (e.g., parsing 'STF' as a date rather than the Supremo Tribunal Federal) but have no UI mechanism to correct the machine's interpretation without rewriting the entire query.
objections.suggested_fix: Make interpreted query components interactive: allow users to click 'X' on incorrect inferred filters to remove them, or click chips to edit the interpretation. Add an 'Advanced search' toggle exposing raw boolean query syntax (AND/OR/NEAR) for precision control required in legal research.

objections.description: Persistent navigation limited to 'Home' and 'Search' is insufficient for investigation-grade workflows. Legal research is project-centric (case files, audit engagements). The current shell provides no quick access to saved document collections, active matters, or shared team workspaces, forcing users to rely on browser bookmarks or external tools to organize research.
objections.suggested_fix: Extend the left rail to include a 'Current Project' selector with collapsible sections for 'Saved Documents', 'Saved Searches', and 'Team Annotations' relevant to the active matter. Replace generic navigation with context-aware legal workspace navigation.

suggestions.description: Implement side-by-side document comparison (redline/diff view) for comparing amended versions of the same act. Essential for tracking legislative changes over time and understanding the evolution of specific articles.

Add citation network analysis showing citing and cited documents with treatment indicators (affirmed, distinguished, criticized, overruled). Legal validity depends not just on textual status but on judicial treatment; researchers need Shepard's-style validation to ensure precedents remain good law.

Add 'Search within results' capability to allow iterative refinement of large result sets without losing the initial query context. Critical for narrowing broad initial searches (e.g., all 'Leis' from 2020) by secondary keywords.
